title: Pre-Market Briefing - 2026-05-28
markdown:
# Pre-Market Briefing

## 1. Market Tone
- **Confirmed:** U.S. markets were mildly positive in the prior session: S&P 500 +0.02%, Nasdaq +0.07%, Dow +0.36% (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** Early futures were weaker, with S&P 500 futures down 0.1% and Nasdaq futures down 0.3% as oil stayed above $90 and Iran-related strikes renewed inflation concerns (The Motley Fool).
- **Interpretation:** Tape tone is cautious-to-neutral: index levels are stable, but macro pressure from oil/inflation is keeping risk appetite restrained.

## 2. Macro And Calendar
- **Confirmed:** April PCE is due today and is expected to show **3.8% YoY** inflation (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** Fed Governor Lisa Cook said she believes rates should be held steady, but she is prepared for a hike if inflationary pressures persist (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee warned about inflation not proving temporary and flagged the risk of consumer spending being boosted by market wealth effects (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** Oil remains above $90 per barrel, with renewed strikes on Iran cited as a pressure point (The Motley Fool).
- **Watch:** No other macro releases or scheduled events were provided in the source.

## 3. Earnings And Company News
- **Confirmed:** **SNOW** jumped nearly 35% pre-market after Q1 revenue rose 34% and beat guidance; management also raised full-year revenue guidance to **$5.84B** from a prior target, citing AI and enterprise partnership momentum (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** **SNOW** announced a new multi-year **$6B** deal with Amazon Web Services to access Graviton compute and AI services (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** **BRZE** reported 30% Q1 revenue growth and raised full-year revenue guidance to **$895M–$899M**, but the stock was down about 8% early after flat earnings (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** **SNPS** beat Q2 revenue expectations with revenue up 42% YoY, but the stock dipped around 2% pre-market as GAAP EPS fell sharply due to one-off costs (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** **P** posted 35% YoY Q1 sales growth to $1.05B, but the stock fell more than 8% on weaker Q2 guidance (The Motley Fool).
- **Confirmed:** **TBBB** was down around 3.5% pre-market after reporting a steep net loss, though operating cash flow was up 64% (The Motley Fool).
- **Upcoming after close today:** **COST**, **ADSK**, and **OKTA** are scheduled to report after today’s close (The Motley Fool).

## 4. Top Movers And Catalysts
- **SNOW** — strongest pre-market reaction on the page; catalyst is the combination of a large revenue beat, raised guidance, AI adoption metrics, and the AWS deal.
- **BRZE** — revenue growth and raised guidance, but stock reaction is negative, suggesting investors may be focused on earnings quality/margins or expectations.
- **P** — sales growth was solid, but weaker Q2 guidance drove a selloff.
- **TBBB** — mixed operating cash flow vs. net loss; pre-market weakness suggests the market is discounting the loss headline.
- **SNPS** — revenue beat, but EPS noise from one-off costs and a modest pre-market fade.

## 5. Ranked Buy Candidates
### 1) **SNOW**
- **Catalyst:** Q1 revenue rose 34% and beat guidance; management raised full-year revenue guidance to **$5.84B**; AI adoption metrics were strong; and the company announced a **$6B** multi-year AWS deal (The Motley Fool).
- **Why it might matter for a swing trade:** This is the clearest momentum setup in the email. Big pre-market gap and multiple confirmatory catalysts can attract follow-through if the market believes AI demand is durable.
- **What would invalidate the idea:** Failure to hold the gap after the open, a quick reversal on heavy volume, or signs the move is fully priced in.
- **What must be checked before entry:** Opening range behavior, whether the gap holds above key intraday support, and whether volume remains supportive rather than exhaustion-like.
- **Pass if:** The stock opens strong, holds above early VWAP/opening range, and does not immediately fade from the pre-market spike.

### 2) **BRZE** as a lower-confidence long watch only
- **Catalyst:** Q1 revenue grew 30%, and management raised full-year revenue guidance to **$895M–$899M** (The Motley Fool).
- **Why it might matter for a swing trade:** The fundamental print is solid enough to justify a rebound attempt if the selloff is mainly sentiment-driven.
- **What would invalidate the idea:** If the market keeps rejecting the stock after the open and the down move continues without support.
- **What must be checked before entry:** Whether the stock can stabilize after the initial drop and reclaim early intraday levels.
- **Pass if:** It finds support, reclaims VWAP, and shows a clear reversal from the post-earnings weakness.
- **Note:** This is much weaker than **SNOW** because the source does not explain why the stock is down on an otherwise strong revenue/guidance report.

## 6. Swing Setup Board
| Ticker | Type | Setup | Trigger | Stop/Invalidation | First Target Logic | Sizing Caution | Pass / No-Trade |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **SNOW** | Catalyst Continuation | Strong earnings + AI/AWS deal momentum | Hold pre-market gains and base above opening range/VWAP | Lose opening range / sharp fade from gap | Prior highs / measured move from gap continuation | Smaller size due to large gap risk | Pass only if gap holds and volume supports continuation |
| **BRZE** | Reversal Bounce | Strong revenue/guidance, but stock sold off | Reclaim VWAP after opening weakness | Fail to reclaim VWAP or break morning low | Mid-gap fill / prior resistance | Size down; post-earnings reversals can be noisy | Pass only if it stabilizes and turns up cleanly |
| **P** | Risk Watch | Good sales, weaker guidance | None yet | No clear bullish trigger from source | N/A | Avoid chasing a weak post-earnings reaction | No-trade unless a clean reversal develops |
| **SNPS** | Risk Watch | Revenue beat, EPS distorted by one-offs | None yet | Continued fade after open | N/A | Not enough in source for a clean swing thesis | No-trade unless market re-prices the beat positively |
| **TBBB** | Risk Watch | Cash flow vs. loss divergence | None yet | Continued weakness on loss headline | N/A | High uncertainty due to conflicting signals | No-trade from this source alone |

## 7. Setup Watch
- **SNOW** — Best active-trader candidate if the gap holds.
- **BRZE** — Possible reversal bounce, but only if price action confirms.
- **COST**, **ADSK**, **OKTA** — Earnings after the close; potential next-session gap candidates, but no setup yet.

## 8. Risk Watch
- **Inflation / oil risk:** PCE and oil above $90 could keep rate expectations and equity multiples under pressure (The Motley Fool).
- **Fed risk:** Fed commentary suggests no easy pivot if inflation persists (The Motley Fool).
- **Gap-fade risk:** **SNOW** is highly extended pre-market; large gap leaders can reverse hard at the open.
- **Negative reaction risk:** **BRZE**, **P**, and **SNPS** show that strong top-line results do not guarantee positive price action.

## 9. Sector And Theme Watch
- **AI / cloud infrastructure:** **SNOW** is the clearest AI-related strength story, with customer adoption and AWS partnership cited (The Motley Fool).
- **Enterprise software:** **SNOW**, **BRZE**, **SNPS**, and **OKTA** all sit in the software tape, with mixed post-earnings reactions.
- **Inflation-sensitive / energy-linked macro theme:** Oil above $90 is feeding a broader inflation concern that may matter for growth multiples (The Motley Fool).
- **Consumer / retail watch:** **COST** is on deck after the close, but no pre-open thesis was provided beyond prior sales trends.

## 10. Worth Reading
- **SNOW** coverage for the AI adoption details and AWS deal framing (The Motley Fool).
- Macro section on PCE, Fed comments, and oil/inflation pressure (The Motley Fool).
- Upcoming earnings preview for **COST**, **ADSK**, and **OKTA** after the close (The Motley Fool).

## 11. Open Questions
- Will **SNOW** hold its pre-market gap after the open, or fade once traders lock in gains?
- Is the **SNOW** move being driven more by fundamentals or by headline excitement around AI/AWS?
- Why is **BRZE** down despite a solid revenue beat and raised guidance?
- Are investors focusing more on **SNPS** GAAP EPS noise than the revenue beat?
- Does the April PCE print reinforce the inflation/oil concern already weighing on futures?

## Notable Noise
- **P**: strong sales, but weaker guidance makes the stock hard to treat as a swing long from this email alone.
- **SNPS**: revenue beat is real, but EPS distortion and only a mild pre-market dip do not create a clean setup.
- **TBBB**: operating cash flow strength is interesting, but the source gives too little context to build a trade.
- **COST**, **ADSK**, **OKTA**: important later, but no actionable pre-market setup yet.

AI Generated Morning Read

Pre-Market Briefing

1. Market Tone

  • Confirmed: U.S. markets were mildly positive in the prior session: S&P 500 +0.02%, Nasdaq +0.07%, Dow +0.36% (The Motley Fool).
  • Confirmed: Early futures were weaker, with S&P 500 futures down 0.1% and Nasdaq futures down 0.3% as oil stayed above $90 and Iran-related strikes renewed inflation concerns (The Motley Fool).
  • Interpretation: Tape tone is cautious-to-neutral: index levels are stable, but macro pressure from oil/inflation is keeping risk appetite restrained.

  • Confirmed: Oil remains above $90 per barrel, with renewed strikes on Iran cited as a pressure point (The Motley Fool).
  • Watch: No other macro releases or scheduled events were provided in the source.

  • SNPS — revenue beat, but EPS noise from one-off costs and a modest pre-market fade.

  • Why is BRZE down despite a solid revenue beat and raised guidance?

  • Does the April PCE print reinforce the inflation/oil concern already weighing on futures?

Notable Noise

  • P: strong sales, but weaker guidance makes the stock hard to treat as a swing long from this email alone.
  • SNPS: revenue beat is real, but EPS distortion and only a mild pre-market dip do not create a clean setup.
  • TBBB: operating cash flow strength is interesting, but the source gives too little context to build a trade.
  • COST, ADSK, OKTA: important later, but no actionable pre-market setup yet.
